title: Verb To Be — Am, Is, Are (Easy)
description: Practise choosing the correct present form of 'to be' (am, is, are) with clear subjects.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
tips: I → am
He / She / It → is
You / We / They → are

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ a student.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am a student.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: She _____ my sister.
questions.explanation: We use 'is' with he, she, and it: She is my sister.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: They _____ from Brazil.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: They are from Brazil.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: He _____ a doctor.
questions.explanation: We use 'is' with he, she, and it: He is a doctor.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: We _____ happy today.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: We are happy today.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: It _____ cold outside.
questions.explanation: We use 'is' with he, she, and it: It is cold outside.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: You _____ my best friend.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: You are my best friend.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ tired after school.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am tired after school.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The cat _____ on the table.
questions.hint: 'The cat' is the same as which pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'The cat' = it, so we use 'is': The cat is on the table.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: My parents _____ at home.
questions.hint: 'My parents' is the same as which pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'My parents' = they, so we use 'are': My parents are at home.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: She _____ a teacher.
questions.explanation: We use 'is' with he, she, and it: She is a teacher.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: We _____ in the classroom.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: We are in the classroom.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ from Spain.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am from Spain.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: He _____ tall.
questions.explanation: We use 'is' with he, she, and it: He is tall.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: They _____ in the park.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: They are in the park.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The book _____ on the desk.
questions.hint: 'The book' is one thing — which pronoun is that?
questions.explanation: 'The book' = it, so we use 'is': The book is on the desk.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: You _____ very kind.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they: You are very kind.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: My brother _____ ten years old.
questions.hint: 'My brother' is the same as which pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'My brother' = he, so we use 'is': My brother is ten years old.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The children _____ at school.
questions.hint: 'The children' is the same as which pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'The children' = they, so we use 'are': The children are at school.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ hungry.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am hungry.

title: Verb To Be — Am, Is, Are (Intermediate)
description: Practise the verb 'to be' with proper nouns, compound subjects, and varied contexts.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
tips: I → am
He / She / It / singular noun → is
You / We / They / plural noun → are

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: Maria _____ from Mexico.
questions.explanation: 'Maria' is a singular name (= she), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: My friends _____ very funny.
questions.hint: 'My friends' is more than one person.
questions.explanation: 'My friends' is plural (= they), so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ 25 years old.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am 25 years old.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The weather _____ nice today.
questions.explanation: 'The weather' = it, so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: Tom and Lucy _____ brother and sister.
questions.explanation: 'Tom and Lucy' = they, so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The museum _____ closed on Mondays.
questions.explanation: 'The museum' is singular (= it), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: You _____ a good singer.
questions.explanation: We always use 'are' with 'you'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The dogs _____ in the garden.
questions.explanation: 'The dogs' is plural (= they), so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: My mother _____ a nurse.
questions.explanation: 'My mother' = she, so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ ready for the test.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am ready for the test.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: These shoes _____ too small.
questions.hint: 'These shoes' — is it singular or plural?
questions.explanation: 'These shoes' is plural (= they), so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: Mr Johnson _____ our English teacher.
questions.explanation: 'Mr Johnson' is singular (= he), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: We _____ late for class.
questions.explanation: We use 'are' with you, we, and they.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The pizza _____ delicious.
questions.explanation: 'The pizza' is singular (= it), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: Anna and I _____ in the same class.
questions.hint: 'Anna and I' means two people — like 'we'.
questions.explanation: 'Anna and I' = we, so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: This room _____ very big.
questions.explanation: 'This room' is singular (= it), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: I _____ interested in music.
questions.explanation: We always use 'am' with 'I': I am interested in music.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: The bananas _____ on the table.
questions.explanation: 'The bananas' is plural (= they), so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: My phone _____ in my bag.
questions.explanation: 'My phone' is singular (= it), so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, or are.
questions.text: You and your sister _____ very tall.
questions.explanation: 'You and your sister' = you (plural), so we use 'are'.

title: Verb To Be — Am, Is, Are, Was, Were (Advanced)
description: Mixed present and past forms of 'to be' with compound subjects and time markers.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
tips: Present: I am, he/she/it is, you/we/they are
Past: I/he/she/it was, you/we/they were
Look for time words like 'yesterday', 'now', 'last week'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: I _____ at school yesterday.
questions.hint: Look at the time word — is it present or past?
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' tells us it is the past. I + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: The students _____ in the library right now.
questions.hint: 'Right now' — is this present or past?
questions.explanation: 'Right now' = present. 'The students' = they, so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: She _____ tired last night.
questions.explanation: 'Last night' = past. She + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: We _____ happy about the good news.
questions.hint: No past time word — what tense is this?
questions.explanation: No past time marker, so this is present. We + present = are.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: The film _____ very boring last weekend.
questions.explanation: 'Last weekend' = past. 'The film' = it, so we use 'was'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: I _____ a teacher now.
questions.explanation: 'Now' = present. I + present = am.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: My grandparents _____ young in the 1970s.
questions.explanation: 'In the 1970s' = past. 'My grandparents' = they, so we use 'were'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: The shop _____ open today.
questions.hint: 'Today' can be present — look at the context.
questions.explanation: 'Today' = present. 'The shop' = it, so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: You _____ at the party last Friday.
questions.explanation: 'Last Friday' = past. You + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: It _____ sunny this morning.
questions.hint: 'This morning' can be present or recent past.
questions.explanation: 'This morning' can be present or past. Both 'is' and 'was' are correct.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: The children _____ at the park two hours ago.
questions.explanation: 'Ago' always means past. 'The children' = they, so we use 'were'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: My sister _____ a student at university.
questions.hint: No past time word — what tense?
questions.explanation: No past time marker = present. 'My sister' = she, so we use 'is'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: I _____ very nervous before the exam yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'Yesterday' = past. I + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: My colleagues _____ friendly and helpful.
questions.hint: No past time word — what tense?
questions.explanation: No past time marker = present. 'My colleagues' = they, so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: The hotel _____ expensive when we visited.
questions.hint: 'When we visited' — what tense is this?
questions.explanation: 'When we visited' = past. 'The hotel' = it, so we use 'was'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: I _____ ready. Let's go!
questions.explanation: This is present. I + present = am.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: Tom and I _____ in the same team last year.
questions.explanation: 'Last year' = past. 'Tom and I' = we, so we use 'were'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: These flowers _____ beautiful.
questions.hint: No past time word, and 'these' suggests now.
questions.explanation: 'These' suggests present. 'Flowers' is plural (= they), so we use 'are'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: He _____ absent from class three days ago.
questions.explanation: 'Ago' = past. He + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb 'to be': am, is, are, was, or were.
questions.text: You _____ welcome to join us.
questions.hint: Is this an invitation for now?
questions.explanation: This is a present invitation. You + present = are.

title: Verb To Be — Contractions (Basic)
description: Practise short forms of the verb 'to be': I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
tips: I am → I'm
He is / She is / It is → He's / She's / It's
You are / We are / They are → You're / We're / They're

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: My name is Alex. _____ a student at this school.
questions.hint: The speaker is talking about himself/herself. I + am = ?
questions.explanation: I am = I'm. 'I'm a student at this school.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Tom is my brother. _____ from England.
questions.explanation: He is = He's. 'He's from England.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: My parents worked late. _____ very tired today.
questions.hint: More than one person. They + are = ?
questions.explanation: They are = They're. 'They're very tired today.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: This is Anna. _____ my best friend.
questions.explanation: She is = She's. 'She's my best friend.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: _____ a beautiful day!
questions.hint: Talking about the day or weather. It + is = ?
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's a beautiful day!'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: You and I are classmates. _____ in the same class.
questions.hint: The speaker and another person. We + are = ?
questions.explanation: We are = We're. 'We're in the same class.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Sam, _____ very good at football.
questions.hint: The speaker is talking to someone. You + are = ?
questions.explanation: You are = You're. 'You're very good at football.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Today is my birthday. _____ 30 years old.
questions.hint: The speaker is talking about his/her age.
questions.explanation: I am = I'm. 'I'm 30 years old.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: The library near my house is open Monday to Saturday. _____ closed on Sundays.
questions.hint: Talking about a shop or place. It + is = ?
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's closed on Sundays.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: My sisters are shopping. _____ at the supermarket.
questions.hint: More than one person at a place.
questions.explanation: They are = They're. 'They're at the supermarket.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Everyone likes David. _____ a really nice person.
questions.explanation: He is = He's. 'He's a really nice person.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: We washed our hands. _____ ready for dinner.
questions.hint: A group including the speaker. We + are = ?
questions.explanation: We are = We're. 'We're ready for dinner.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Please ask me your question. _____ happy to help you.
questions.explanation: I am = I'm. 'I'm happy to help you.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: _____ really cold outside.
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's really cold outside.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Sorry, Ben, but _____ late for the meeting.
questions.hint: The speaker is talking to someone who arrived late.
questions.explanation: You are = You're. 'You're late for the meeting.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: My aunt works long hours. _____ a doctor at the hospital.
questions.explanation: She is = She's. 'She's a doctor at the hospital.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: My cousins are in Spain. _____ on holiday this week.
questions.hint: More than one person on holiday.
questions.explanation: They are = They're. 'They're on holiday this week.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: I packed my suitcase last night. _____ so excited about the trip!
questions.explanation: I am = I'm. 'I'm so excited about the trip!'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: _____ a very old building.
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's a very old building.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction of the verb 'to be' (I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're).
questions.text: Hello, we live next door. _____ the new neighbours.
questions.explanation: We are = We're. 'We're the new neighbours.'

title: Verb To Be — Contractions (Advanced)
description: Practise both positive and negative contractions: I'm, isn't, aren't, he's not, we're not.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
tips: Positive: I'm, you're, he's, she's, it's, we're, they're
Negative: isn't (= is not), aren't (= are not)
Special: I'm not (NOT 'I amn't')

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: She _____ at home. She's at work.
questions.hint: Use a negative short form after 'she'.
questions.explanation: Is not = isn't. 'She isn't at home.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: I missed you. _____ so happy to see you!
questions.hint: The speaker is happy — positive form.
questions.explanation: I am = I'm. 'I'm so happy to see you!'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: They _____ ready yet. Give them five minutes.
questions.hint: Use a negative short form after 'they'.
questions.explanation: Are not = aren't. 'They aren't ready yet.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: Look at the weather. _____ a sunny day today.
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's a sunny day today.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: The bus _____ here yet. We have to wait.
questions.explanation: Is not = isn't. 'The bus isn't here yet.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: I live in Seoul. _____ from Japan. I'm from Korea.
questions.hint: Use the special negative form for the speaker.
questions.explanation: I am not = I'm not. We never say 'I amn't'.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: The children are outside. _____ in the garden. Can you see them?
questions.hint: People are in the garden — positive.
questions.explanation: They are = They're. 'They're in the garden.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: This exercise _____ difficult. It's easy!
questions.explanation: Is not = isn't. 'This exercise isn't difficult.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: This is Mr Harris. _____ the new teacher. Nice to meet you.
questions.hint: Mr Harris is male. He + is = ?
questions.explanation: He is = He's. 'He's the new teacher.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: We _____ sure about the answer.
questions.hint: Use a negative short form after 'we'.
questions.explanation: Are not = aren't. 'We aren't sure about the answer.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: This room has no air conditioning. _____ very hot in here. Can you open a window?
questions.explanation: It is = It's. 'It's very hot in here.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: You _____ alone. I'm here with you.
questions.hint: Use a negative short form after 'you'.
questions.explanation: Are not = aren't. 'You aren't alone.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: Come on, Anna! You and I need to hurry. _____ late again! The teacher will be angry.
questions.hint: The speaker and Anna are late. We + are = ?
questions.explanation: We are = We're. 'We're late again!'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: My phone _____ working. I need a new one.
questions.explanation: Is not = isn't. 'My phone isn't working.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: Please don't make food for me. _____ hungry. I just had lunch.
questions.explanation: I am not = I'm not. 'I'm not hungry.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: My grandmother makes wonderful meals. _____ really good at cooking.
questions.explanation: She is = She's. 'She's really good at cooking.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: Everyone loves Lisa's music. _____ the most popular singer right now.
questions.explanation: She is = She's. 'She's the most popular singer right now.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: The small mistake is easy to fix. _____ a problem. Don't worry.
questions.explanation: It is not = It isn't / It's not. 'It isn't a problem.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: Jack _____ always late for school.
questions.hint: Jack is male. Which positive contraction fits?
questions.explanation: He is = He's. 'He's always late for school.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct contraction. Use positive contractions (I'm, he's, we're) or negative contractions (isn't, aren't, I'm not).
questions.text: The Tigers have won every game. _____ the best team in the league this year.
questions.hint: Talking about a team's achievement — positive.
questions.explanation: They are = They're. 'They're the best team in the league this year.'

title: Verb To Be — Negative Forms (Basic)
description: Practise making negative sentences with am not, isn't, and aren't.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
tips: I am not (I'm not)
He / She / It is not (isn't)
You / We / They are not (aren't)

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: I _____ tired. I feel great!
questions.hint: I + not. Which form of 'be' goes with 'I'?
questions.explanation: I + am + not = I am not / I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: She _____ at school today. She is sick.
questions.explanation: She + is not = She isn't / She's not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: We _____ hungry. We ate one hour ago.
questions.explanation: We + are not = We aren't / We're not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: The shop _____ open. It closes at 5 pm.
questions.explanation: The shop (= it) + is not = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: They _____ from Italy. They are from Spain.
questions.explanation: They + are not = They aren't / They're not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: It _____ cold today. The sun is shining.
questions.explanation: It + is not = It isn't / It's not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: You _____ late. You are right on time.
questions.explanation: You + are not = You aren't / You're not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: He _____ a teacher. He is a doctor.
questions.explanation: He + is not = He isn't / He's not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: I _____ happy about the result.
questions.hint: I + am + not. Remember: we never say 'amn't'.
questions.explanation: I + am not = I am not / I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: My parents _____ at home. They are at work.
questions.explanation: They + are not = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: The film _____ interesting. It is boring.
questions.explanation: It + is not = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: We _____ ready to go. Please wait.
questions.explanation: We + are not = We aren't / We're not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: The children _____ quiet. They are very noisy.
questions.explanation: They + are not = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: This book _____ mine. It belongs to Tom.
questions.explanation: It + is not = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: I _____ afraid of dogs. I love them!
questions.explanation: I + am not = I am not / I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: He _____ married. He is single.
questions.explanation: He + is not = He isn't / He's not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: You _____ wrong. You are correct!
questions.explanation: You + are not = You aren't / You're not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: The coffee _____ hot. It is cold.
questions.explanation: It + is not = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: My friends _____ here yet. They are coming later.
questions.explanation: They + are not = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form of the verb 'to be': am not, is not (isn't), or are not (aren't).
questions.text: I _____ sure about the answer.
questions.explanation: I + am not = I am not / I'm not.

title: Verb To Be — Negative Forms (Advanced)
description: Practise present and past negative forms: isn't, aren't, wasn't, weren't.
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
tips: Present: am not, isn't, aren't
Past: wasn't (= was not), weren't (= were not)
Look for time words to decide present or past

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The train _____ on time yesterday. It arrived late.
questions.hint: 'Yesterday' = past. The train (= it) + was + not = ?
questions.explanation: Past + singular = wasn't. 'The train wasn't on time yesterday.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: We _____ at the party last Saturday.
questions.explanation: Past + we = weren't. 'We weren't at the party last Saturday.'

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The shops _____ open right now. They close at 6 pm.
questions.hint: 'Right now' = present. The shops (= they) + are + not = ?
questions.explanation: Present + plural = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: I _____ at home when you called.
questions.hint: 'When you called' = past. I + was + not = ?
questions.explanation: Past + I = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: She _____ happy about the news today.
questions.explanation: Present + she = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: They _____ at school last Monday. They were sick.
questions.explanation: Past + they = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The weather _____ very good at the moment.
questions.hint: 'At the moment' = present. The weather (= it) = ?
questions.explanation: Present + it = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: He _____ in the office last week. He was on holiday.
questions.explanation: Past + he = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: I _____ interested in that film. Let's watch something else.
questions.explanation: I am not = I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The students _____ quiet during the exam yesterday.
questions.hint: 'Yesterday' = past. The students (= they) + were + not = ?
questions.explanation: Past + they = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: You _____ at the meeting this morning.
questions.explanation: 'This morning' usually refers to past. You + were + not = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The road _____ safe. Be careful!
questions.hint: A warning about now — present tense.
questions.explanation: Present + singular = isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: My grandparents _____ born in this city. They came from a village.
questions.hint: 'Born' refers to the past. My grandparents = they.
questions.explanation: Past + they = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The test _____ easy. Many students failed.
questions.hint: 'Many students failed' = past. The test (= it) = ?
questions.explanation: Past + it = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: Those answers _____ correct. Please try again.
questions.hint: 'Please try again' = present. Those answers = they.
questions.explanation: Present + they = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: She _____ here when the accident happened.
questions.explanation: Past + she = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: The rooms _____ clean. We need to tidy up.
questions.hint: 'We need to tidy up' = present situation.
questions.explanation: Present + plural = aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: I _____ at the concert last night. I stayed at home.
questions.explanation: Past + I = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: It _____ his fault. Don't blame him.
questions.hint: Could be present or past — both are possible.
questions.explanation: Both 'isn't' (present) and 'wasn't' (past) work here.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct negative form: am not, isn't, aren't, wasn't, or weren't.
questions.text: We _____ surprised when he arrived. We expected him.
questions.hint: 'When he arrived' = past. We + were + not = ?
questions.explanation: Past + we = weren't.

title: Verb To Be — Questions
description: Practise forming questions with am, is, are, was, and were.
instructions: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
tips: Questions swap the subject and verb: She is → Is she...?
Present: Am I? Is he/she/it? Are you/we/they?
Past: Was I/he/she/it? Were you/we/they?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ you a student?
questions.explanation: Questions with 'you' use 'Are': Are you a student?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ she from France?
questions.explanation: Questions with 'she' use 'Is': Is she from France?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ they at home yesterday?
questions.explanation: Past + they = Were: Were they at home yesterday?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ I late for the meeting?
questions.explanation: Questions with 'I' use 'Am': Am I late?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ it cold outside?
questions.explanation: Questions with 'it' use 'Is': Is it cold outside?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ the children happy at school?
questions.explanation: Present + they = Are: Are the children happy?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ he at work last Monday?
questions.explanation: Past + he = Was: Was he at work last Monday?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ you tired after the game?
questions.hint: You + ? No clear time word — both present and past work.
questions.explanation: Both 'Are you tired?' (now) and 'Were you tired?' (after) work.

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ the film good last night?
questions.explanation: Past + it = Was: Was the film good last night?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ we in the right place?
questions.explanation: Present + we = Are: Are we in the right place?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ your parents at the concert last weekend?
questions.explanation: Past + they = Were.

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ this your first time here?
questions.explanation: Present + it = Is: Is this your first time?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ you at the party two days ago?
questions.explanation: Past + you = Were.

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ your brother a doctor?
questions.explanation: Present + he = Is: Is your brother a doctor?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ I right or wrong?
questions.explanation: Present + I = Am: Am I right or wrong?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ the food ready?
questions.explanation: Present + it = Is: Is the food ready?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ it expensive when you bought it?
questions.explanation: Past + it = Was.

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ the teachers nice at your old school?
questions.explanation: Past + they = Were.

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ there a pharmacy near here?
questions.explanation: Present + singular = Is: Is there a pharmacy?

questions.instruction: Complete each question with the correct form of the verb 'to be': Am, Is, Are, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ you happy with your new job?
questions.explanation: Present + you = Are: Are you happy?

title: Verb To Be — Short Answers
description: Practise short answers with the verb 'to be': Yes, I am. / No, she isn't. / Yes, they were.
instructions: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
tips: Positive: Yes, I am / Yes, she is / Yes, they are / Yes, he was / Yes, we were
Negative: No, I'm not / No, she isn't / No, they aren't / No, he wasn't / No, we weren't
NEVER contract positive short answers: Yes, I am (NOT Yes, I'm)

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are you a student? — Yes, I _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'I'. No contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, I am. (NOT 'Yes, I'm.')

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Is she your sister? — No, she _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, she isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are they at home? — Yes, they _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'they'. No contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, they are. (NOT 'Yes, they're.')

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Is it cold outside? — No, it _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, it isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Was he at the party? — Yes, he _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'he' — past tense.
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, he was.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are you hungry? — No, I _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Were they late? — No, they _____.
questions.hint: Negative short answer — past tense with 'they'.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, they weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Is your brother tall? — Yes, he _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'he'. No contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, he is. (NOT 'Yes, he's.')

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Were you at school yesterday? — Yes, I _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer — past tense with 'I'.
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, I was.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Is this your book? — No, it _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, it isn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are we late? — No, we _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, we aren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Was the exam difficult? — Yes, it _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer — past tense with 'it'.
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, it was.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are your friends nice? — Yes, they _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'they'. No contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, they are.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Was she happy? — No, she _____.
questions.hint: Negative short answer — past tense with 'she'.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, she wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Am I in the right room? — Yes, you _____.
questions.hint: 'Am I...?' → Answer with 'you'. Positive, no contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, you are.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Were the tickets expensive? — No, they _____.
questions.hint: Negative short answer — past tense with 'they'.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, they weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Is he a good teacher? — Yes, he _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer with 'he'. No contraction!
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, he is.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Are you ready? — No, I _____.
questions.explanation: Negative short answer: No, I'm not.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Was it a good film? — Yes, it _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer — past tense with 'it'.
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, it was.

questions.instruction: Complete each short answer. Remember: no contractions in positive short answers (Yes, I am — NOT Yes, I'm).
questions.text: Were you surprised? — Yes, I _____.
questions.hint: Positive short answer — past tense. 'You' → answer with 'I'.
questions.explanation: Positive short answer: Yes, I was.

title: Verb To Be — Was & Were (Past Positive)
description: Practise the past forms of 'to be': was and were in positive sentences.
tips: I / He / She / It → was
You / We / They → were
Look for past time words: yesterday, last week, ago

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: I _____ at home yesterday.
questions.hint: Which form goes with 'I' in the past?
questions.explanation: I + past = was: I was at home yesterday.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: They _____ very happy about the news.
questions.hint: Which form goes with 'they' in the past?
questions.explanation: They + past = were: They were very happy.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: She _____ a great singer when she was young.
questions.explanation: She + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: We _____ in London last summer.
questions.explanation: We + past = were: We were in London last summer.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: The film _____ really funny.
questions.explanation: It + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: You _____ right about the answer.
questions.explanation: You + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: He _____ born in 1995.
questions.explanation: He + past = was: He was born in 1995.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: The students _____ in the library all morning.
questions.explanation: They + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: It _____ very cold last winter.
questions.explanation: It + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: My parents _____ teachers before they retired.
questions.explanation: They + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: I _____ very nervous before my first day at work.
questions.explanation: I + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: The party _____ amazing! Everyone had fun.
questions.explanation: It + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: Tom and Sarah _____ in the same class at university.
questions.explanation: They + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: She _____ the best player on the team.
questions.explanation: She + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: We _____ friends for many years.
questions.explanation: We + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: The hotel _____ near the beach.
questions.explanation: It + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: You _____ very kind to help me.
questions.explanation: You + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: The shops _____ open until 9 pm.
questions.explanation: They + past = were.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: He _____ tired after the long journey.
questions.explanation: He + past = was.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with 'was' or 'were'.
questions.text: My grandmother _____ a very strong woman.
questions.explanation: She + past = was.

title: Verb To Be — Past Negative & Questions
description: Practise wasn't, weren't and past questions: Was she...? Were they...?
instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
tips: Negative: wasn't (I/he/she/it), weren't (you/we/they)
Questions: Was + I/he/she/it? Were + you/we/they?
Negative questions: Wasn't he...? Weren't they...?

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: The food _____ very good at that restaurant. We didn't enjoy it.
questions.explanation: It + was not = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ you at the cinema last night?
questions.explanation: Past question + you = Were you...?

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: They _____ at work on Saturday. The office was closed.
questions.explanation: They + were not = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ she happy when she heard the news?
questions.explanation: Past question + she = Was she...?

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: I _____ feeling well yesterday, so I stayed in bed.
questions.explanation: I + was not = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ the children quiet during the film?
questions.explanation: Past question + they = Were...?

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: He _____ at the meeting because he was on holiday.
questions.explanation: He + was not = wasn't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ it expensive to travel there?
questions.explanation: Past question + it = Was it...?

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: We _____ surprised because we already knew.
questions.explanation: We + were not = weren't.

questions.instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct past form: was, were, wasn't, weren't, Was, or Were.
questions.text: _____ there many people at the concert?
questions.explanation: Past question + plural = Were there...?

questions.text: The shops _____ open when we arrived. It was too early.
